markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Close ED-WB-005 + ED-WB-006: side-by-side + inline diff of two document versions (history from MT-156 backend) for prose (block-level semantic diff) and code blocks (Monaco diff editor, bundled); CRDT-aware merge/conflict resolution UI for the MT-174 conflict path: pick-mine/pick-theirs/merge-per-block, never silent data loss - resolves the H5 conflict-reload-discards-edits finding at the UX level.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.
Bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries only (Tiptap/Monaco precedent); no CDN; no external app launches as product behavior.
PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; UI artifacts are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle (v02.193), product code, tests, and receipts.

OPERATOR ORDER (DEC-007, 2026-06-12): NO scaffolding, templates, mock-ups, or vibecoding; every feature must be working AND tested in runtime (executed vitest/Playwright/real-PG proof per feature, not status text). A feature without an executed runtime proof is NOT done.
Merge must never discard local edits without explicit per-block operator choice (negative test required).
scope.expected_behavior: Operator diffs any two versions and resolves save conflicts per block; nothing is lost silently.
acceptance_criteria: Prose block diff + Monaco code diff runtime-proven.
Conflict merge flow: local edits preserved through resolution (negative test: no silent discard).
History-pair selection works against real backend history.
Adversarial self-review recorded per the WP loop; findings fixed before evidence write.
verification: pnpm vitest run green; tsc --noEmit clean; eslint clean.
Offline Playwright runtime proof for each named capability (zero external network requests).
handoff_requirements: List files changed, tests run with exact names/counts, commits.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence and Playwright artifacts produced.
List remaining risks and any honestly-documented deviations (e.g., prose multi-cursor approach).

patterns_found: Monaco provides a built-in diff editor API for side-by-side code diffs.

ProseMirror changesets model inserted/deleted ranges for presentation, but current MT-247 can stay Handshake-native by computing block-level semantic diffs over existing rich document JSON instead of introducing an outside authority.
VS Code merge UX separates current, incoming, and result views, and requires explicit accept-current/accept-incoming/combination choices before completing a merge.
evidence.selected_approach: Implement the first MT-247 slice with TDD: a Handshake-native rich-document diff/merge model that produces prose block diffs, code-block diff inputs for Monaco, and explicit per-block merge choices that preserve local edits unless the operator chooses otherwise.
rejected_options: No external diff/merge service or sidecar authority.
No blind text-level replace across the whole rich document because it risks losing structured block semantics and code-block metadata.
validation_plan: RED/GREEN unit tests for prose block diff, code-block diff pair extraction, and no-silent-discard merge choices.
Then wire UI/runtime proof only after the model contract is green.

behavior_delivered: History pair selectors lazily load selected version bodies through loadRichDocumentVersion and render a prose block diff plus Monaco-ready code diff rows.
Rich document diff/merge model computes prose and monacoCodeBlock changes over structured rich document JSON.
Conflict snapshot merge UI requires explicit per-block operator choices and applies the selected mine/theirs/merged result without silently discarding local edits.
A real Monaco diff editor is mounted in an offline Playwright harness with zero external network requests.

pnpm --dir app exec eslint src/lib/editor/document_diff_merge.ts src/lib/editor/document_diff_merge.test.ts src/components/RichDocumentView.tsx src/components/RichDocumentView.test.tsx src/lib/api.ts src/lib/monaco/setup.ts src/harness/rich_document_diff_harness.tsx

verification_runs.result: PASS: 70 files, 670 tests. Output includes pre-existing unrelated jsdom canvas, React act, and TextSelection warnings.

verification_runs.result: PASS: 1 Playwright test; real .monaco-diff-editor visible; zero external requests; Vite emitted chunk-size warnings only.
PASS: 1 backend API test; proves paginated history omits bodies, lazy history/:doc_version returns selected content_json, missing version returns 404. Rust compile emitted existing warnings.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Prose block diff + Monaco code diff runtime-proven.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: document_diff_merge unit coverage, RichDocumentView history pair test, and offline Playwright harness mounting real Monaco diff editor.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Conflict merge flow preserves local edits and prevents silent discard.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: RichDocumentView conflict merge test and merge-plan model require explicit choices for conflicting blocks.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: History-pair selection works against real backend history.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: Frontend loadRichDocumentVersion binding plus backend knowledge_documents_api_tests::mt156_history_is_paginated_and_omits_version_bodies passing against PostgreSQL-backed test path.
adversarial_self_review evidence record appended in this lifecycle update.

deviations_and_notes: No EventLedger write evidence was produced by MT-247 because the diff selector reads existing append-only history; PostgreSQL-backed backend history proof was executed.
The backend proof required a minimal compile unblock: making sqlx::QueryBuilder import unconditional in postgres.rs because unconditional code used QueryBuilder while the import was cfg-gated.
The product and governance worktrees contain unrelated dirty changes; this evidence is scoped to MT-247 files and the listed compile unblock.

review_checks: Verified frontend loadRichDocumentVersion calls the existing /knowledge/documents/:document_id/history/:doc_version route and RichDocumentView calls it for both selected versions.
Verified same-version diff is blocked by canLoadHistoryDiff and the load button disabled state; loader also returns when versions are equal or missing.
Verified runtime Monaco proof asserts a visible .monaco-diff-editor rather than only static <pre> code markup.
Verified Playwright harness blocks non-loopback network and records zero external requests.
Verified conflict merge uses explicit choices before applying and has a regression test for explicit merge choice behavior.
Verified the real backend history endpoint test passes after the QueryBuilder import unblock.
counterfactuals_considered: If the lazy backend history route were removed or changed, the frontend selector would fail to load version bodies and the backend API test would fail.
If the Monaco proof regressed to static markup, the Playwright .monaco-diff-editor visibility assertion would fail.
If unresolved conflicts could be applied silently, the conflict merge unit coverage would fail and local edits would be at risk.
If same-version comparisons were allowed, the disabled-state/source inspection would expose the regression and a validator should add a direct negative UI test.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Full frontend Vitest run is green but stderr contains unrelated pre-existing warnings.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Do not treat warning-clean output as proven for MT-247; validator should track warning cleanup separately if needed.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Rust backend test compiles with many pre-existing warnings.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: The MT-247 dependency unblock fixed the blocking compile error only; warning cleanup is outside this closure unit.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: The Playwright proof is an offline harness rather than a full live app navigation through a running backend.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Frontend unit tests prove the component behavior, backend PostgreSQL test proves the real history endpoint, and offline Playwright proves real Monaco rendering with network isolation; validator can add a full live-app smoke if desired.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Narrow rustfmt check on postgres.rs reports pre-existing file-wide formatting/import-order drift.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Do not auto-rustfmt the large dirty file as part of MT-247; the backend API test compiles and passes after the QueryBuilder import unblock. Validator can schedule a separate Rust formatting cleanup.

validation_needed_next: Validator should rerun the listed focused frontend, Playwright, and backend commands from a clean session.
Validator should inspect the saved Playwright screenshot and confirm the prose row plus Monaco code diff are visible/readable.
Validator should keep lifecycle status out of PASS until independent validation completes.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger and/or offline Playwright where the contract names durable state or GUI behavior.
